title: Airlines Band Together to Create Bill Gates-Backed Sustainable Aviation Fuel Fund
content:
A group of leading airlines including Alaska Airlines, American Airlines, IAG, Cathay Pacific, Japan Airlines and Singapore Airlines have launched the $150 million oneworld BEV Fund, managed by Bill Gates-backed Breakthrough Energy Ventures. The fund aims to accelerate next-generation sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) technologies to cut aviationâ€™s 2â€“3% share of global emissions.

Breakthrough Energy Ventures plans to back scalable, low-cost solutions such as algae-based fuels or fuels produced using hydrogen and carbon dioxide, while moving beyond traditional biofuel methods from fats, oils and sugars, which remain costly and difficult to scale. The initiative reflects airlinesâ€™ net-zero targets and the urgent need to boost SAF supply, which accounted for only 0.3% of global jet fuel production in 2024.

The fund complements airlinesâ€™ existing investments in SAF startups like Infinium and LanzaJet but pools capital to make scaling more feasible. By jointly investing, airlines hope to reduce costs, attract private capital, and â€œleapfrogâ€ current technologies to build a future supply of affordable, low-carbon jet fuel.
author: Analysts Account
